finding_results.notes: Replaced the composed thesis-speech exchange (lines 183-199 in snapshot) with a rougher, evidence-grounded version. Noor now puts specific messages side by side on screen and quotes them directly ('I'm here to support your work during the sprint' vs 'If anything in that area still seems off, please flag it'), providing concrete textual comparison. She also checks the knife delivery note against the others, finding the same tone with less text. Three messy turns introduced: self-correction at line 187 ('It doesn't — look'), abandoned phrasing at line 191 ('But the — ' followed by head shake), and a stopped sentence at line 199 ('the building — ' where she redirects to the document instead of finishing). The composed global summary of Ember's operating model ('nudges, leaks, delivers something sharp') is removed; the Haruki-vs-Diego distinction now arrives through specific concrete comparison rather than polished abstraction. Both must-land beats preserved: register consistency across Ember's messages (lines 185-195), and the decision to map public/private/action/interval (line 205). Suki's voice remains interrogative and direct; Noor's remains observational with self-correction under pressure. Contraction levels match style bible profiles for both characters.

summary: Single finding addressed. The Noor/Suki analysis scene (lines 183-205) was revised to replace composed thesis-speech with friction-laden dialogue grounded in concrete message comparison. Noor now reads and quotes specific Ember messages on screen, compares their structure and tone directly, and arrives at partial conclusions through self-corrections and abandoned sentences rather than polished inferences. The two must-land beats (Ember's register does not change across contexts; the pair decides to build a public/private/action/interval mapping) are preserved. No changes outside the target passage; heading contract, continuity, setups (ventilation_control, ember_communication_analysis), and character voice signatures intact.
